name: Glazed Irish Tea Cake
recipeInstructions:
PREHEAT OVEN TO 325°F, with rack in the center of the oven.
Generously grease a 9-inch loaf pan. Dust with flour; tap pan over sink to discard excess flour. Cut parchment paper or waxed paper to fit the bottom of the pan. Set aside.
FOR CAKE:
1. Use a mixer to cream butter, sugar, and vanilla until fluffy.
2. Add eggs, one at a time, beating each until fluffy.
3. Add cream cheese. Mix until well combined.
4. Sift flour, baking powder, and salt together.
5. Place currants in a small bowl. Add 1/4 cup of the flour mixture to currants. Stir currants until well coated.
6. Add remaining flour to batter, alternating with buttermilk. Mix until smooth.
7. Use a wooden spoon to stir in currants and all of the flour. Stir until well combined.
8. Transfer batter to prepared pan. Smooth surface with spatula.
9. Bake until well-browned and toothpick inserted into center comes out clean, about 1 hour and 25 minutes.
CAKE RESTING:
Let cake rest in pan for 10 minutes before removing it from the pan.
GLAZE APPLICATION:
1. Use a flexible metal spatula to separate the cake from the sides of the pan.
2. Carefully remove cake to cooling rack.
3. Spread glaze on warm cake.
4. Let cake cool completely.
5. Store cake in foil at room temperature for up to 3 days, or freeze wrapped airtight for up to 3 months.
FOR GLAZE:
Combine sugar and lemon juice in a small bowl. Stir until smooth.
